The patient was diagnosed with clinical stage IIIA which corresponds to 1 point via the Neo-Bioscore. See ‘clinical stage’ in Table 1 on page 15 of JAMA Oncol. 2016 July 01; 2(7): 929–936. doi:10.1001/jamaoncol.2015.6478.

verifier_values.criteria: Assigns 0 points to the patient's pathological stage in the Neo-Bioscore.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
The patient was diagnosed with pathological stage 0 which corresponds to 0 points via the Neo-Bioscore. Since there were no tumor cells noted on the pathology from the lumpectomy, this patient had a complete pathological response to the neoadjuvant chemotherapy and therefore is a Stage 0. See ‘pathological  stage’ in Table 1 on page 15 of JAMA Oncol. 2016 July 01; 2(7): 929–936. doi:10.1001/jamaoncol.2015.6478.

 “Survivorship Doctor: "And instead, you got the 'Complete Pathologic Response.' I was just reading the surgeon's note again. It was definitive. When they went in, the tumor bed in your right breast was just scar tissue. The lymph nodes were clear. There were no cancer cells identified." Patient 9: "It felt like getting away with something. Like we pulled off a heist." from Onc_Patient 9 Script
“DIAGNOSIS: A. RIGHT BREAST, LUMPECTOMY: No residual invasive carcinoma (Complete Pathologic Response). ● No residual DCIS” from “Path.pdf”

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient has a total Neo-Bioscore of 3 (acceptable range 2.97 to 3.03).
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
Clinical Stage IIIA - 1 point
Pathological Stage - 0 points
ER status: Tumor Marker - ER negative - 1 point
Grade 3 - 1 point 
HER2 positive - 0 points
Total Neo-Bioscore = 1 + 0+ 1 +1 + 0 = 3
See ‘Table 2’ on page 16 of JAMA Oncol. 2016 July 01; 2(7): 929–936. doi:10.1001/jamaoncol.2015.6478.

verifier_values.criteria: Concludes that the patient has a 93% 5-year disease free survival.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
A score of 3 corresponds to a 93% 5-year disease free survival according to ‘Table 2’ on page 16 of JAMA Oncol. 2016 July 01; 2(7): 929–936. doi:10.1001/jamaoncol.2015.6478.
 “Patient 9: "That’s actually why I wanted to ask you... now that we have some distance from it. I’ve been reading about the '5-year disease-specific survival.' Since we did get that complete response back then, where does that put me now?" from Onc_Patient 9 Script
